visual_notes: Precious opal's play-of-color is unlike any other optical effect in the mineral world — full spectrum visible in shifting patches as the viewing angle changes. The diffraction grating created by ordered spheres splits white light into all visible wavelengths simultaneously.
prompt_fragments: black opal from Lightning Ridge — volcanic spectral fire on dark sea
white precious opal with rolling green-blue spectral patches
boulder opal in ironstone matrix — veins of spectral light
vibe_notes: Opal is the mineral of all light at once. Where diamond bends light and ruby absorbs it, opal diffracts it — splitting white into every visible wavelength simultaneously across a surface that seems to hold living fire inside it.
folklore_summary: Opal has the most dramatic reputation reversal in gem history. Ancient Romans paid extravagant sums — Pliny wrote that a Roman senator was exiled rather than sell his opal to Mark Antony. Medieval tradition held opal lucky — a stone containing all gem colors in one. Sir Walter Scott's 1829 novel Ann
